suggested_rules: Corridors on the same street in the same borough with overlapping or identical limits should be merged when one is a specific treatment (busway) of the same physical corridor segment.
suggestion.rationale: Both records describe the same physical corridor segment on 34th Street in Manhattan between Ninth Avenue and Third Avenue. The validator found they share identity key 'corridor_34th-st-busway'. The busway_34thstreet source (describing the 34th Street Busway) was submitted to corridor_34th-street, and corridor_34th-street already has corridor_34th-st-busway as an alias. The corridor_34th-st-busway record's merged limits already include the simpler 'Ninth Avenue to Third Avenue' form. The busway is a regulatory treatment applied to this same physical corridor, not a different corridor. The directional limit detail in corridor_34th-st-busway (eastbound/westbound) is a refinement of the same limits, not a different scope.
